reviewTrigger: signal, state, authority, proof, cost, false-positive, convergence, stopping, escalation, rollback, or accepted-outcome change

records.signal: Push or pull request for main with the exact source revision as candidate identity.
records.stateOwner: GitHub run/check identity externally; workflow concurrency desired source locally.
records.durableState: One run/check per repository and ref; newer candidates cancel stale work.
records.principal: Ephemeral GitHub Actions job token with contents read only.
records.operation: Run bounded repository verification without provider mutation.
records.resource: crcorbett/bundjil candidate source and CI check.
records.environment: GitHub-hosted CI; local parity is a separate environment.
records.convergence: One result for the current candidate; stale work cancels.
records.proof: Source SHA, run/check identity, bounded command result, report path/digest, and exit postcondition when externally observed.
records.stopping: Stop on failure, unavailable proof, timeout, widened permission, or unexpected external access.
records.rollback: Revert workflow desired source or separately disable it under GitHub-setting authority.
records.acceptedOutcome: The exact candidate receives one bounded verification disposition; activity alone is not success.

metrics.acceptedOutcome: Accepted candidate outcomes by source SHA.
metrics.falsePositive: Checks rejected after an unchanged rerun or fixture-backed correction.
metrics.feedbackLatency: Candidate signal to first actionable result.

records.publication: Read-only check publication is bounded to the exact candidate; no repository/provider mutation.
records.reviewTrigger: Workflow, check, command, permission, runner, timeout, false-positive, or latency change.
records.retirement: Retire when it no longer prevents its named failures or a smaller earlier gate proves the same candidate.
records.externalReadback: Unavailable in HGI-306 and therefore inconclusive; HGI-309 owns authenticated settings/run readback.
records.carryingCost: Hosted duration, maintenance, false-positive review, and human attention per accepted candidate.
records.nonClaim: Local parity and source desired state do not prove a hosted run, token, check, or accepted candidate.

records.signal: Main push plus exact hgi-304-v1 authority epoch.
records.stateOwner: Changesets version-PR state and GitHub pull request when externally enabled.
records.durableState: Serialized non-cancelling writer targeting at most one current version pull request.
records.principal: Job-scoped GITHUB_TOKEN limited to contents and pull-request write when separately enabled.
records.operation: Create or converge a version pull request only; no publish, merge, approve, tag, release, or deploy.
records.resource: crcorbett/bundjil refs/heads/main and one Changesets version pull request.
records.environment: GitHub Actions, disabled pending authenticated epoch/settings readback.
records.duration: One serialized run, at most 20 minutes.
records.convergence: Changesets owns one version pull request; in-flight writer is never cancelled.
records.proof: Source SHA, run identity, generated pull request, sanitized package summary, and repository readback if enabled.
records.stopping: Stop on absent epoch, target drift, duplicate writer, unexpected write, publication input, or missing rollback.
records.escalation: Repository owner and release authority owner.
records.rollback: Disable epoch gate and close/revert the generated pull request under separate authority.
records.acceptedOutcome: One reviewed version pull request exists for the exact main candidate, without publication.

metrics.acceptedOutcome: Accepted version pull requests, not workflow runs.
metrics.falsePositive: No-op or incorrect version pull requests.
metrics.feedbackLatency: Main signal to reviewable pull request.
metrics.synchronousHumanAttention: Version review and merge decision time.

records.publication: External write remains disabled until separate authority and readback; merge is always human.
records.reviewTrigger: Epoch, token, settings, target, Changesets, package, cost, duplicate, or publication change.
records.retirement: Retire if no release workflow is needed or accepted outcomes do not justify its write authority/cost.
records.externalReadback: Unavailable and inconclusive in HGI-306; no release capability is claimed.
records.carryingCost: Writer runtime, pull-request review, false versions, and release-owner attention.
records.nonClaim: Repository source does not prove the epoch variable, token permission, run, pull request, or release.

records.signal: One exact owner-authored comment beginning with @claude.
records.stateOwner: One issue/pull-request conversation and one invocation-scoped run.
records.durableState: No autonomous queue; newer owner invocation cancels stale work.
records.principal: Owner-gated workflow with read-oriented permissions and provider-managed OIDC input.
records.operation: One read-only foreground assistance request using Read, Glob, and Grep.
records.resource: The exact issue or pull-request conversation.
records.convergence: One invocation ends after one provider-managed response or bounded failure.
records.proof: Source/run/conversation identity and sanitized response disposition if externally observed.
records.stopping: Reject non-owner, indirect/quoted mention, unknown target, write request, or unbounded tool.
records.escalation: Repository owner and Anthropic integration owner.
records.rollback: Cancel or separately disable the workflow; no continuous state to reconcile.
records.acceptedOutcome: The owner receives one useful bounded response; invocation count alone is not value.

metrics.falsePositive: Irrelevant or incorrect findings accepted for review.

records.publication: No automatic review/comment loop or downstream mutation is admitted.
records.reviewTrigger: Actor gate, trigger, tool list, permissions, provider, cost, quality, or timeout change.
records.retirement: Retire if bounded foreground value does not justify cost or authority surface.
records.externalReadback: OIDC, secret binding, action behavior, and response actuality remain HGI-309-owned and inconclusive.
records.carryingCost: Per-invocation provider cost plus owner attention and false-positive review.
records.nonClaim: Interactive source does not prove provider identity, credential, response, comment, quality, or cost.

records.signal: Former pull-request open/synchronize loop; no current signal is accepted.
records.stateOwner: Retirement provenance in HGI-308/HGI-304 and exact workflow inventory policy.
records.durableState: No workflow path; restoration requires a new accepted SPEC and admission epoch.
records.operation: No automatic review, comment, approval, merge, deploy, release, or provider action.
records.environment: Repository desired source only; historical GitHub state is not inferred.
records.proof: Absent workflow path, retired authority record, HGI-308 decision, HGI-304 receipt, and negative fixtures.
records.stopping: Any restoration attempt stops unless authority, severity, deduplication, convergence, proof, cost, false-positive, rollback, and escalation evidence is accepted.
records.rollback: Remove an unapproved restored workflow and retain the failed admission evidence.
records.acceptedOutcome: No ungoverned review comment or repeated loop is emitted.

metrics.acceptedOutcome: Absence of ungoverned outcomes; future epoch must measure accepted findings.
metrics.falsePositive: Future epoch must record reviewer disposition per finding.

records.reviewTrigger: A new SPEC proposes restoration with complete admission evidence.
records.retirement: Already retired; archive only after all historical provenance remains reachable.
records.externalReadback: No claim is made about historical runs or external integrations.
records.carryingCost: Small inventory/negative-fixture cost; no provider runtime cost.
records.nonClaim: Retirement source does not prove GitHub disabled every historical run or integration.

records.signal: One approved immutable candidate and exact target-owned deployment procedure.
records.stateOwner: Vercel deployment/alias state externally; app runbooks and proof packets locally.
records.durableState: One candidate, target, previous alias, rollback identity, approval, and before/after readback.
records.principal: Authenticated deployment operator under target-specific authority.
records.operation: Build, deploy, promote, prove, or roll back only the exact approved target.
records.resource: Named Vercel project, immutable deployment, stable alias, and previous deployment.
records.duration: One foreground procedure; no schedule or autonomous retry.
records.convergence: Exact alias/readback postcondition or rollback to named previous deployment.
records.proof: Target-owned before/after metadata and journey packet bound to immutable candidate.
records.stopping: Stop on missing authority/readback, target drift, ambiguous alias, failed journey, or uncertain consequence.
records.escalation: Target app operator, Vercel owner, and incident owner.
records.rollback: Execute the target-owned rollback runbook under separate authority and verify readback.
records.acceptedOutcome: The exact consumer journey is proved on the named target or the prior target is restored.

metrics.acceptedOutcome: Accepted deployments by journey, not build count.
metrics.falsePositive: Deployments that passed local/preflight but failed target journey.

records.publication: No scheduled deployment writer; each mutation requires separate approval and readback.
records.reviewTrigger: Project, alias, config, entrypoint, runbook, proof, rollback, or authority change.
records.retirement: Retire a deployment path when the app/target no longer exists or a safer owned platform replaces it.
records.externalReadback: Not performed in HGI-306; deployment and alias state remain inconclusive.
records.carryingCost: Operator attention, platform duration/cost, proof, rollback readiness, and incident risk.
records.nonClaim: Runbooks, source, builds, and local preflight do not prove a Vercel deployment, alias, health, or Production state.

records.signal: One signed Sendblue inbound webhook for the exact configured route.
records.stateOwner: Durable Upstash replay/lease records and Sendblue message/provider state.
records.durableState: Conversation key, replay record, lease/fencing state, turn identity, and bounded result.
records.principal: Verified webhook ingress plus bounded agent runtime service identity.
records.operation: Verify, deduplicate, process one inbound turn, and schedule at most one authorised response.
records.resource: Exact webhook route, conversation, replay key, and message turn.
records.environment: Configured agent target; current external state requires readback.
records.duration: One bounded webhook turn with lease expiry and no blind replay.
records.convergence: Replay and atomic lease state prevent duplicate consumer-visible turns.
records.proof: Signature/auth classification, replay/lease receipt, turn result, provider acceptance, and separate delivery readback when claimed.
records.stopping: Stop on invalid signature, replay conflict, lease loss, uncertain send consequence, provider failure, or missing rollback/escalation.
records.escalation: Agent operator, Sendblue owner, persistence owner, and incident owner.
records.rollback: Stop ingress/send, preserve replay evidence, revoke exact route/bypass if authorised, and reconcile before resume.
records.acceptedOutcome: One inbound message produces at most one correctly classified turn and no duplicate external response.

metrics.acceptedOutcome: Accepted consumer-visible turns with zero duplicates.
metrics.falsePositive: Valid messages rejected or duplicate classification errors.

records.publication: Runtime response remains within the admitted app contract; provider configuration changes require separate authority.
records.reviewTrigger: Webhook, signature, replay, lease, channel, model, response, provider, or incident change.
records.retirement: Retire when the channel is removed or a replacement proves equal idempotence, authority, proof, and recovery.
records.externalReadback: HGI-306 performs none; current route, credential, webhook, message, and delivery state remain inconclusive.
records.carryingCost: Runtime/provider cost, replay storage, monitoring, false rejection, duplicate risk, and incident attention.
records.nonClaim: Local tests and source do not prove a current Sendblue webhook, account, message, processing, or handset delivery.

records.signal: One exact approved message operation or admitted inbound turn.
records.stateOwner: Turn/replay state locally and provider message state externally.
records.durableState: One idempotency/turn identity and one bounded provider consequence receipt.
records.principal: Agent channel service under exact message authority.
records.operation: Schedule one message; never blindly retry an uncertain consequence.
records.resource: Exact line, recipient/conversation, message turn, and provider handle.
records.environment: Approval-gated provider target or admitted inbound response path.
records.duration: One bounded send attempt and readback window.
records.convergence: One consequence identity; uncertain result stops for reconciliation.
records.proof: Provider acceptance plus separate processing/delivery readback for stronger claims.
records.stopping: Stop on ambiguous target/authority, timeout after possible attempt, provider rejection, or unavailable readback.
records.escalation: Agent operator and Sendblue account owner.
records.rollback: Do not duplicate; reconcile provider state and use the target runbook for any corrective action.
records.acceptedOutcome: The exact approved message consequence is observed at the claimed boundary without duplicates.

metrics.falsePositive: Acceptance represented as delivery or valid sends rejected.

records.publication: No scheduled bulk sender or self-authorized retry loop.
records.reviewTrigger: Provider API, target, message contract, idempotency, retry, cost, or delivery proof change.
records.retirement: Retire when outbound messaging is removed or a safer owner replaces it.
records.carryingCost: Provider charges, false delivery claims, duplicate risk, and reconciliation attention.
records.nonClaim: HTTP acceptance is not processing, delivery, handset visibility, or recipient receipt.

records.signal: One exact task-scoped Photon project reconciliation operation.
records.stateOwner: Photon project platform/line/webhook state and the protected rollout receipt.
records.durableState: Stable resource identities, before/after sanitized inventory, operation, billing classification, and rollback owner.
records.principal: Authenticated rollout operator using the approved project-scoped credential.
records.operation: List, adopt, create, update, or delete only the exact platform, line, or webhook required by the rollout.
records.resource: Approved Photon project, iMessage platform, one dedicated line, and exact environment webhook.
records.environment: Task-scoped Preview or Production rollout stage.
records.duration: One stage-bounded operation with immediate readback and revocation.
records.convergence: List before create; stable-ID adoption; ambiguous writes reconcile without blind retry.
records.proof: Authenticated before/after inventory, operation result, billing consequence, postcondition, and rollback identity.
records.stopping: Stop on project drift, ambiguity, secret loss, billing mismatch, unavailable readback, or partial failure.
records.escalation: Photon project owner, agent operator, and billing owner.
records.rollback: Delete only rollout-created resources by stable ID, restore only rollout-changed platform state, and read back the inventory.
records.acceptedOutcome: The exact desired rollout resource topology is observed without changing unrelated project state.

metrics.falsePositive: Operations reported converged before stable readback.
metrics.synchronousHumanAttention: Approval, billing, and ambiguity reconciliation attention.

records.publication: No standing reconciler or scheduled writer; each mutation requires the active task approval receipt.
records.reviewTrigger: Photon API, project, platform, line, webhook, billing, adoption, retry, rollback, or authority change.
records.retirement: Retire after the rollout or when a separately accepted declarative owner replaces it.
records.externalReadback: Pending the active Photon reconciliation task; current target state is inconclusive.
records.carryingCost: Line subscription, provider limits, operator attention, secret handling, and rollback readiness.
records.nonClaim: Source, approval, or local proof does not establish current Photon topology, billing, delivery, typing, or Production.

records.signal: One signed Photon inbound webhook for the exact environment route.
records.stateOwner: Fresh Channel replay/lease records plus Photon webhook/message state.
records.durableState: Webhook/message replay identity, lease/fencing state, turn identity, and bounded result.
records.principal: Verified Photon webhook plus the bounded agent runtime service identity.
records.operation: Verify, deduplicate, process one inbound turn, and schedule at most one authorized response.
records.resource: Exact webhook, Space, message replay key, route, and turn.
records.environment: Explicit Preview or Production deployment and fresh namespace.
records.duration: One bounded webhook turn with lease expiry and no blind replay.
records.convergence: Stable event identity and atomic replay state prevent duplicate consumer-visible turns.
records.proof: Signature/timestamp classification, replay receipt, turn result, provider acceptance, and separate handset observation when claimed.
records.stopping: Stop on invalid signature, stale timestamp, route/namespace drift, replay conflict, lease loss, or uncertain send.
records.escalation: Photon owner, agent operator, persistence owner, and incident owner.
records.rollback: Stop ingress/send, preserve replay evidence, drain retries, delete the exact webhook under management authority, and reconcile before resume.
records.acceptedOutcome: One inbound message produces at most one correctly classified turn and no duplicate external response.

metrics.acceptedOutcome: Accepted consumer-visible turns with zero duplicates.
metrics.falsePositive: Valid deliveries rejected or duplicate classification errors.

records.publication: Runtime response remains within the admitted app contract; provider configuration changes require photon-management authority.
records.reviewTrigger: Webhook, signature, retry, replay, lease, route, SDK, provider, or incident change.
records.retirement: Retire when Photon is removed or a replacement proves equal idempotence, authority, proof, and recovery.
records.externalReadback: Pending the hosted Preview task; current route, webhook, message, and delivery state is inconclusive.
records.carryingCost: Runtime/provider cost, replay storage, monitoring, duplicate risk, and incident attention.
records.nonClaim: A local signed fixture or HTTP 2xx does not prove hosted Photon ingress, Eve completion, handset delivery, or Production.

records.signal: One approved Photon message or typing start/stop operation.
records.stateOwner: Channel replay state locally and Photon message/typing state externally.
records.durableState: One turn/consequence identity and one bounded provider outcome receipt.
records.principal: Agent Channel service using the environment-scoped Photon credential and pinned SDK.
records.operation: Send one message or typing transition; never blindly retry an uncertain consequence.
records.resource: Exact project, line, Space, turn, and message or typing action.
records.environment: Approval-gated Preview proof or Production admitted-response path.
records.duration: One bounded SDK attempt and readback window.
records.convergence: One consequence identity; uncertain result stops for reconciliation.
records.proof: Decoded provider outcome plus separate processing, delivery, or handset display observation for stronger claims.
records.stopping: Stop on ambiguous line/Space, timeout after possible attempt, provider rejection, release failure, or unavailable readback.
records.escalation: Agent operator and Photon project owner.
records.rollback: Do not duplicate; stop further operations, reconcile provider state, and use the target runbook for corrective action.
records.acceptedOutcome: The exact message or typing consequence is accepted at the claimed boundary without duplicates.

metrics.acceptedOutcome: Accepted outcomes by consequence identity and action.
metrics.falsePositive: Acceptance represented as delivery/display or valid operations rejected.

records.publication: No scheduled sender, typing loop, or self-authorized retry.
records.reviewTrigger: SDK, line/Space selection, message, typing, timeout, release, cost, or proof change.
records.retirement: Retire when Photon outbound is removed or a safer owner replaces it.
records.externalReadback: Pending the hosted Preview task; outbound, typing, and handset state is inconclusive.
records.carryingCost: Provider charges, false delivery/display claims, duplicate risk, and reconciliation attention.
records.nonClaim: SDK acceptance or no-op is not handset delivery, typing display, or recipient receipt.

records.signal: One authenticated read request or one later direct exact-owner decision for a matching pending call.
records.stateOwner: Executor pending-call identity and target-owned downstream state.
records.durableState: One exact toolkit/call/operation/resource/environment plus approval and replay state.
records.principal: Authenticated Executor/toolkit owner and separately authenticated approver.
records.operation: Read named toolkit state or resume exactly one approved pending call.
records.resource: Exact toolkit, pending call, and downstream resource.
records.duration: One foreground read or resume; no same-turn/repeated resume.
records.convergence: Pending call settles once and downstream consequence is independently read back.
records.proof: Authenticated target readback, approval receipt, call result, and downstream consequence/rollback receipt.
records.stopping: Stop on unavailable identity/readback, quoted/ambiguous approval, settled/replayed call, or uncertain downstream consequence.
records.escalation: Toolkit owner, approver, downstream owner, and incident owner.
records.rollback: Use the downstream target runbook; Executor success alone is not rollback proof.
records.acceptedOutcome: One exact operation settles once and its downstream consequence matches the approved claim.

metrics.acceptedOutcome: Accepted settled operations with downstream readback.
metrics.falsePositive: Incorrect approval classification or false consequence claims.
metrics.feedbackLatency: Pending call to later decision and readback.

records.publication: No scheduled resume or tool-derived authority; each consequential operation is separately approved.
records.reviewTrigger: Toolkit, protocol, identity, approval, resume, replay, downstream target, or rollback change.
records.retirement: Retire model-mode workaround when native/browser authorization provides stronger equivalent proof.
records.externalReadback: Not performed in HGI-306; toolkit/call actuality remains inconclusive.
records.carryingCost: Approval attention, toolkit latency/cost, replay controls, and downstream proof.
records.nonClaim: Tool discovery, protocol text, success output, or quoted approval is not operation authority or downstream proof.

records.signal: One local or approved deployed Eve interaction.
records.stateOwner: Eve session/stream state and AI Gateway account/model state externally.
records.durableState: One session/turn identity with bounded stream outcome and no fabricated completion.
records.principal: Configured Eve runtime under the exact environment/account authority.
records.operation: Execute one model turn and classify success/failure without provider mutation.
records.resource: Exact Eve session, account/project/model, and turn.
records.duration: One foreground turn; stream failure ends the claim.
records.convergence: One turn outcome; interrupted recovery remains deferred until deterministic proof exists.
records.proof: Session/stream receipt, exact model-source classification, and target readback when externally claimed.
records.stopping: Stop on unknown account/model, missing cost boundary, auth ambiguity, stream failure, or requested consequential operation.
records.escalation: Agent operator and AI Gateway account owner.
records.rollback: Return to the retained model source/config and preserve failed turn evidence.
records.acceptedOutcome: One real turn completes at the claimed boundary without fabricated output.
records.publication: No scheduled model work or provider/account mutation.
records.reviewTrigger: Model source, account, session, stream, cost, recovery, or deployed target change.
records.retirement: Retire a model path when unsupported or a smaller proven path replaces it.
records.carryingCost: Model usage, latency, quality review, and failure recovery.
records.nonClaim: Configured source and local tests do not prove account/model access, cost, response, recovery, or Production.

records.signal: One explicitly configured private proxy model request after hosted auth/storage qualification.
records.stateOwner: Proxy request/session and encrypted profile/fence/refresh state.
records.durableState: Exact profile key/version, refresh fence, request identity, and bounded SSE result.
records.principal: Private proxy service and trusted profile owner.
records.operation: Serve one private model request; no public access or raw token/profile exposure.
records.resource: Exact proxy deployment, encrypted profile, bearer boundary, and request.
records.environment: Disabled for hosted use pending HGI-309 qualification.
records.duration: One request/refresh lease with bounded output and no blind replay.
records.convergence: Single-flight refresh/fence and one response identity.
records.proof: Stored-profile, auth, refresh, SSE, deployment, and live journey receipts at matching targets.
records.stopping: Stop on missing profile/auth/readback, reauthentication, fence loss, output close, or uncertain provider consequence.
records.escalation: Proxy operator, profile owner, provider owner, and incident owner.
records.rollback: Disable proxy mode, revoke/rotate exact credentials if authorized, and restore retained model source.
records.acceptedOutcome: One authorized private request completes without credential leakage or duplicate provider consequence.

metrics.falsePositive: Auth/reauth classification or unusable response errors.

records.publication: No hosted enablement until separate authority, deployment, and readback proof.
records.reviewTrigger: Profile, cipher, auth, bearer, refresh, fence, provider, deployment, or mode change.
records.retirement: Retire when unsupported or a native trusted auth path replaces it with equal proof.
records.carryingCost: Encrypted storage, refresh coordination, provider use, security review, and reauthentication attention.
records.nonClaim: Local mock/stored-profile tests do not prove hosted auth, provider access, response, or Production.

records.signal: A bounded scheduled or foreground scan observes a possible stale current-owner claim.
records.stateOwner: Typed immutable candidate under docs/documentation-audit/freshness-candidates; never a current owner.
records.durableState: Candidate identity binds generator, source path/digest/revision/time, finding, proposed owner, expiry, review, publication, quarantine, and recovery.
records.principal: Report generator with no repository edit, review, approval, or publication authority.
records.operation: Emit or refresh an isolated candidate only; no automatic source edit or policy publication.
records.resource: Exact observed source revision and proposed owner paths; candidate root is excluded from default context and self-input.
records.duration: One bounded scan/candidate; no continuous mutation loop.
records.convergence: Same source digest/revision and finding identity produce one candidate; candidate output cannot feed the next scan.
records.proof: Schema-valid candidate, source digest/revision, exclusion checks, reviewer decision, and separate atomic publication/readback only if later authorized.
records.stopping: Stop on missing owner/source/provenance, self-source, identity collision, target drift, contradiction, missing reviewer/publisher, or readback mismatch.
records.escalation: Documentation owner and separately authorized publisher.
records.rollback: Quarantine/retract candidate or restore last-known-good owner; preserve provenance and require a new decision before retry.
records.acceptedOutcome: A reviewer accepts, rejects, or quarantines an evidence-complete candidate with minimal attention; scan activity alone is not success.
records.publication: Not authorized by the generator; requires separate approval, distinct publisher identity, immutable target revision, atomic readback, and recovery receipt.
records.reviewTrigger: Candidate schema, source selection, exclusion, owner, reviewer, publisher, readback, false-positive, or recovery change.
records.retirement: Retire the scanner if accepted outcomes do not justify attention/cost or a generated source owner eliminates the finding class.
records.externalReadback: Not applicable to repository candidate generation; any provider claim remains separately authority-gated.
records.carryingCost: Scan duration, candidate storage, false-positive review, publication attention, and recovery maintenance.
records.nonClaim: A freshness candidate, local check, or model judgment is not a current owner, repository edit, approval, publication, or external-state proof.
